label: Istanbul (Constantinople)
sub: Ottoman capital from 1453; Republic capital until 1923; largest city of modern Turkey

label: Ankara
sub: Modern Turkish capital since 1923; seat of the Grand National Assembly and Atatürk's mausoleum (Anıtkabir)

label: İzmir (Smyrna)
sub: Greek forces land here 1919; Turkish forces recapture September 1922; Great Fire destroys city's cosmopolitan quarters

label: Gallipoli (Çanakkale)
sub: WWI Allied amphibious campaign April–December 1915; Atatürk's defense repels ANZAC & British forces; ~220,000 total deaths

label: Samsun
sub: Atatürk lands here May 19, 1919 to organize national resistance; start of Turkish War of Independence

label: Bursa
sub: First Ottoman capital captured 1326; center of Ottoman silk trade; Grand Mosque (Ulu Cami) built 1399

label: Edirne (Adrianople)
sub: Second Ottoman capital before Constantinople; site of four major Russo-Ottoman/Balkan wars; Selimiye Mosque UNESCO site

label: Diyarbakır
sub: Largest Kurdish-majority city; center of PKK-linked unrest; urban warfare curfews 2015-2016; historic basalt walls UNESCO site

label: Erzurum
sub: Key WWI eastern front city; site of Erzurum Congress (1919) where Atatürk launched national movement; Armenian-Ottoman battleground

label: Kahramanmaraş
sub: Epicenter of Feb 6, 2023 Mw 7.8 earthquake; 53,537 killed across 10 provinces; worst Turkish natural disaster in a century

label: Söğüt
sub: Birthplace of the Ottoman dynasty; Osman I's tribal base c. 1299; annual Ertuğrul Gazi commemoration

label: Ankara — 1960 Coup
sub: Military coup May 27, 1960; Gen. Gürsel deposes Menderes government; PM Menderes executed by hanging September 1961

label: Istanbul — July 15, 2016 Coup
sub: Tanks deployed on Bosphorus bridges; citizens block coup forces; 250+ killed; Erdoğan calls public resistance via FaceTime on TV

label: Taksim Square / Gezi Park
sub: May-June 2013 Gezi Park protests; millions mobilize against Erdoğan government; police crackdown kills 8, injures thousands

label: Hakkari Province
sub: PKK insurgency stronghold; remote mountains bordering Iraq/Iran; Turkish military operations ongoing since 1984

label: Cizre
sub: Urban battles 2015-2016 between Turkish security forces and PKK-affiliated YDG-H; curfews lasting months; historic center destroyed

label: Van
sub: Ancient Armenian city; Armenian Genocide deportations began here; Van rebellion 1915; Kurdish-majority today

label: Konya
sub: Conservative Anatolian heartland; AKP stronghold; home of Rumi (Mevlana); center of Erbakan's Islamist movement in 1990s

label: Hatay (Antakya)
sub: Historic city almost entirely destroyed in Feb 2023 earthquake; ancient site of Antioch; multicultural Christian-Muslim population

label: Sakarya River
sub: Site of Battle of Sakarya Aug-Sep 1921; decisive Turkish nationalist victory over Greek forces; Atatürk awarded 'Gazi' title

label: Trabzon
sub: Black Sea trade hub; birthplace of PM Bülent Ecevit; site of 2007 murder of journalist Hrant Dink's killer's origins; nationalist stronghold

label: Gaziantep
sub: Major city near Syrian border; hub for Syrian refugee operations; received millions of refugees 2011-present; Islamic State bombing 2016

label: Afrin (Syria border area)
sub: Turkish Operation Olive Branch Jan-Mar 2018; captures Kurdish YPG/SDF-held Afrin canton; Turkish-backed forces remain

label: İzmir — Women's Suffrage
sub: Turkey grants women full voting rights in 1934, ahead of France (1944) and Switzerland (1971); İzmir congress voted in favor

label: Sincan (Ankara suburb)
sub: 'Postmodern coup' signal: military drives tanks through Islamist mayor's district Feb 4, 1997; precursor to Erbakan's forced resignation

label: Malazgirt (Manzikert)
sub: Battle of Manzikert, August 26, 1071; Seljuk Sultan Alp Arslan defeats Byzantine Emperor Romanos IV Diogenes; decisive battle that opened Anatolia to permanent Turkish settlement and Ottoman dominance.

label: Kocaeli (İzmit) Earthquake
sub: Mw 7.6 earthquake struck August 17, 1999; 17,000–18,000 killed, 250,000+ left homeless across Marmara region; exposed construction code failures that would recur fatally in 2023.

label: Suruç (Şanlıurfa)
sub: ISIS suicide bombing July 20, 2015 killed 33 young Kurdish activists preparing aid for Kobane; triggered PKK retaliation attacks on Turkish security forces and definitively ended the 2013 peace ceasefire.

label: Dersim (Tunceli)
sub: 1937–38 Turkish military campaign against Alevi-Kurdish Zaza tribes; thousands killed including civilians; province renamed Tunceli. PM Erdoğan acknowledged the killings as a 'massacre' in 2011 — a rare official admission.

label: Sivas — Madımak Massacre
sub: Islamist mob burned the Madımak Hotel on July 2, 1993, killing 35 Alevi intellectuals and 2 hotel workers at the Pir Sultan Abdal cultural festival; a defining trauma for Turkey's 15–20 million Alevi community.

label: Bodrum (Aegean crossing)
sub: Primary Aegean crossing point for Syrian refugees to Greek islands in 2015; Alan Kurdi's body washed ashore near Bodrum September 2, 2015, shocking the world and accelerating EU-Turkey migration talks leading to the March 2016 deal.

label: Ankara Train Station (Garı)
sub: ISIS twin suicide bombings October 10, 2015 killed 102 peace rally participants — the deadliest terrorist attack in Turkish history; HDP-organized rally ahead of November elections.

label: Adıyaman Province
sub: Among the hardest-hit provinces in the February 6, 2023 earthquake; over 7,000 killed in Adıyaman alone; nearly all city-center buildings destroyed; slow rescue response drew fierce criticism from survivors.
